User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the DocuExtract application who uploads and manages documents.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

creationDate.description: The date and time when the user account was created.

lastModifiedDate.description: The date and time when the user account was last modified.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents an uploaded image or document file by a user for text and entity extraction.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who uploaded this document. (Relationship: User 1:N Document)

originalFileName.description: The original file name of the uploaded document.

uploadedAt.description: The date and time when the document was uploaded.

documentType.description: The classified type of the document (e.g., IDENTITY_PROOF, BILLING_STATEMENT, MEDICAL_REPORT, OTHER).

storagePath.description: The URI or path where the original document file is stored.

processingStatus.description: The current processing status of the document (e.g., UPLOADED, PROCESSING, PROCESSED, FAILED).

lastModifiedAt.description: The date and time when the document record was last modified.

ExtractedText.title: ExtractedText
ExtractedText.description: Stores the raw text content extracted from a document using OCR and LLMs.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExtractedText entity.

documentId.description: Reference to the Document from which this text was extracted. (Relationship: Document 1:1 ExtractedText)

content.description: The full text content extracted from the document.

extractedAt.description: The date and time when the text was initially extracted.

lastEditedAt.description: The date and time when the extracted text was last manually edited by a user.

IdentityProofExtraction.title: IdentityProofExtraction
IdentityProofExtraction.description: Stores structured details extracted from identity proof documents (e.g., ID cards, passports).

id.description: Unique identifier for the IdentityProofExtraction entity.

documentId.description: Reference to the Document from which these details were extracted. (Relationship: Document 1:1 IdentityProofExtraction)

fullName.description: The full name of the person as extracted from the document.

dateOfBirth.description: The date of birth of the person.

address.description: The residential address as extracted from the document.

idNumber.description: The identification number (e.g., passport number, driver's license number).

documentIssueDate.description: The date when the identity document was issued.

documentExpiryDate.description: The date when the identity document expires.

extractedAt.description: The date and time when these details were extracted.

lastModifiedAt.description: The date and time when these extracted details were last modified (e.g., via user correction).

BillingStatementExtraction.title: BillingStatementExtraction
BillingStatementExtraction.description: Stores structured details extracted from billing statements and invoices.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BillingStatementExtraction entity.

documentId.description: Reference to the Document from which these billing details were extracted. (Relationship: Document 1:1 BillingStatementExtraction)

vendorName.description: The name of the vendor or service provider on the bill.

invoiceNumber.description: The unique invoice number of the billing statement.

invoiceDate.description: The date when the invoice was issued.

dueDate.description: The date when the payment for the invoice is due.

totalAmount.description: The total amount of the bill.

currency.description: The currency in which the total amount is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR).

extractedAt.description: The date and time when these billing details were extracted.

lastModifiedAt.description: The date and time when these extracted billing details were last modified (e.g., via user correction).

BillingLineItem.title: BillingLineItem
BillingLineItem.description: Represents individual line items within a BillingStatementExtraction.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BillingLineItem entity.

billingStatementExtractionId.description: Reference to the BillingStatementExtraction this line item belongs to. (Relationship: BillingStatementExtraction 1:N BillingLineItem)

description.description: A description of the product or service for this line item.

quantity.description: The quantity of the item or service.

unitPrice.description: The price per unit of the item or service.

totalPrice.description: The total price for this line item (quantity * unitPrice).

extractedAt.description: The date and time when this line item was extracted.

lastModifiedAt.description: The date and time when this line item was last modified (e.g., via user correction).

MedicalReportExtraction.title: MedicalReportExtraction
MedicalReportExtraction.description: Stores structured diagnostic and patient details extracted from hospital or medical reports.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MedicalReportExtraction entity.

documentId.description: Reference to the Document from which these medical details were extracted. (Relationship: Document 1:1 MedicalReportExtraction)

patientName.description: The full name of the patient mentioned in the report.

reportDate.description: The date when the medical report was generated.

diagnoses.description: A list of diagnoses identified in the medical report.

symptoms.description: A list of symptoms reported or observed in the medical report.

prescriptions.description: A list of prescribed medications or treatments.

doctorName.description: The name of the doctor who authored or signed the report.

hospitalName.description: The name of the hospital or clinic where the report was issued.

extractedAt.description: The date and time when these medical details were extracted.

lastModifiedAt.description: The date and time when these extracted medical details were last modified (e.g., via user correction).

definition.description: Top-level collection for all user profiles. Documents are identified by the Firebase Authentication UID. This path forms the root for all user-specific data, ensuring clear ownership and segregation. The 'id' field in the User entity corresponds to the {userId} path parameter.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: A subcollection holding all documents uploaded by a specific user. Each document's 'userId' field must match the parent {userId} in the path for authorization independence and simplified security rules. The 'id' field in the Document entity corresponds to the {documentId} path parameter.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this document.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the uploaded document.

definition.description: A subcollection storing the raw text extracted from a specific document. Each ExtractedText document must include denormalized 'userId' and 'documentId' fields for authorization independence, allowing direct security rule evaluation without parent lookups. The 'id' field corresponds to {extractedTextId}.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent document.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent document.

params.name: extractedTextId
params.description: The unique identifier for the extracted text.

definition.description: A subcollection for structured data extracted from identity proof documents. Each IdentityProofExtraction document must include denormalized 'userId' and 'documentId' fields for authorization independence. The 'id' field corresponds to {identityProofExtractionId}.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent document.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent document.

params.name: identityProofExtractionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the identity proof extraction.

definition.description: A subcollection for structured data extracted from billing statements. Each BillingStatementExtraction document must include denormalized 'userId' and 'documentId' fields for authorization independence. The 'id' field corresponds to {billingStatementExtractionId}.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent document.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent document.

params.name: billingStatementExtractionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the billing statement extraction.

definition.description: A subcollection containing individual line items for a specific billing statement extraction. Each BillingLineItem document must include denormalized 'userId', 'documentId', and 'billingStatementExtractionId' fields for authorization independence. The 'id' field corresponds to {lineItemId}.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the top-level document.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent document.

params.name: billingStatementExtractionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent billing statement extraction.

params.name: lineItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the billing line item.

definition.description: A subcollection for structured data extracted from medical reports. Each MedicalReportExtraction document must include denormalized 'userId' and 'documentId' fields for authorization independence. The 'id' field corresponds to {medicalReportExtractionId}.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent document.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent document.

params.name: medicalReportExtractionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the medical report extraction.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for DocuExtract prioritizes user data segregation and authorization independence, crucial for a personal document management system. All user-specific data is organized under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection. This immediately establishes clear ownership, making security rules simple and efficient by allowing direct comparison of `request.auth.uid` with the `{userId}` wildcard in the path. Documents uploaded by a user are stored in a subcollection at `/users/{userId}/documents/{documentId}`. Each `Document` document itself contains the `userId` field, which is consistent with the path, ensuring that any authorization check can happen directly on the `Document`'s data without requiring a `get()` call to a parent `User` document.

For the various extraction entities (`ExtractedText`, `IdentityProofExtraction`, `BillingStatementExtraction`, `MedicalReportExtraction`), they are designed as subcollections under their respective `Document`s. To achieve **Authorization Independence (CRITICAL)**, each extraction document (e.g., `ExtractedText`) will have the `userId` (and `documentId` for `BillingLineItem`) explicitly denormalized within its own data. For example, a document at `/users/{userId}/documents/{documentId}/extractedTexts/{extractedTextId}` will contain a `userId` field matching the path's `userId` and a `documentId` field matching the path's `documentId`. This denormalization eliminates the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules to check parent document ownership or attributes. Rules can simply evaluate `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` and `resource.data.documentId == {documentId}` directly on the document being accessed, enabling atomic operations and simplifying debugging.

This structure directly supports **Query Against Path Segments (QAPs)** by enforcing **Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture)**. Since all data under `/users/{userId}` belongs exclusively to that user, `list` queries (e.g., to retrieve all documents for a user or all extractions for a document) are inherently secured. The Firestore rules engine can validate the `userId` in the path against `request.auth.uid` for all reads, writes, and lists within these user-scoped collections without needing to filter results at the database level. Each subcollection (e.g., `/extractedTexts`, `/identityProofExtractions`) holds documents of a single type with a consistent security posture, further simplifying rule logic. The use of explicit state modeling (e.g., `processingStatus` in `Document`) and descriptive wildcards (`{userId}`, `{documentId}`) enhances data clarity and predictability, adhering to all core design principles and strategy mandates.
